Calcium, Serum.name: Calcium, Serum
Calcium, Serum.explanation: Measures the amount of calcium in the blood, essential for bone health, muscle function, and nerve signaling.

Creatinine, Serum.name: Creatinine, Serum
Creatinine, Serum.explanation: A waste product from muscle breakdown, used to evaluate kidney function.

Urea, Serum.name: Urea, Serum
Urea, Serum.explanation: Waste product formed in the liver from protein breakdown, excreted by the kidneys.

Blood Urea Nitrogen (BUN).name: Blood Urea Nitrogen
Blood Urea Nitrogen (BUN).explanation: Measures the amount of nitrogen in the blood that comes from urea.

BUN/Creatinine Ratio.name: BUN/Creatinine Ratio
BUN/Creatinine Ratio.explanation: Helps determine the cause of kidney injury or dehydration.

Sodium, Serum.name: Sodium, Serum
Sodium, Serum.explanation: Crucial electrolyte for maintaining fluid balance and nerve function.

Potassium, Serum.name: Potassium, Serum
Potassium, Serum.explanation: Key electrolyte for muscle and heart function.

Chloride, Serum.name: Chloride, Serum
Chloride, Serum.explanation: Works with sodium to maintain fluid balance and blood pressure.

Uric Acid, Serum.name: Uric Acid, Serum
Uric Acid, Serum.explanation: Waste product from the breakdown of purines in food.

Cholesterol - Total.name: Total Cholesterol
Cholesterol - Total.explanation: Overall amount of cholesterol in the blood.

Cholesterol - HDL.name: High-Density Lipoprotein (HDL)
Cholesterol - HDL.explanation: The 'good' cholesterol that helps remove other forms of cholesterol from the bloodstream.

Triglycerides (TGL).name: Triglycerides
Triglycerides (TGL).explanation: A type of fat found in your blood.

Cholesterol - LDL.name: Low-Density Lipoprotein (LDL)
Cholesterol - LDL.explanation: The 'bad' cholesterol that can build up in blood vessels.

Cholesterol - VLDL.name: Very Low-Density Lipoprotein (VLDL)
Cholesterol - VLDL.explanation: Carries triglycerides in the blood; linked to plaque deposits.

LDL/HDL Ratio.name: LDL to HDL Ratio
LDL/HDL Ratio.explanation: Specific risk indicator for cardiovascular disease.

Bilirubin Total.name: Total Bilirubin
Bilirubin Total.explanation: Yellowish substance made during the normal breakdown of red blood cells.

Bilirubin Direct.name: Direct (Conjugated) Bilirubin
Bilirubin Direct.explanation: Bilirubin that has been processed by the liver.

Bilirubin Indirect.name: Indirect (Unconjugated) Bilirubin
Bilirubin Indirect.explanation: Bilirubin before it reaches the liver.

Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP).name: Alkaline Phosphatase
Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP).explanation: An enzyme found mostly in the liver, bones, kidneys, and digestive system.

Alanine Aminotransferase (ALT/SGPT).name: ALT / SGPT
Alanine Aminotransferase (ALT/SGPT).explanation: An enzyme found mainly in the liver, released into blood when the liver is damaged.

Aspartate Aminotransferase (AST/SGOT).name: AST / SGOT
Aspartate Aminotransferase (AST/SGOT).explanation: An enzyme found in the liver, heart, and muscles.

Gamma Glutamyl Transferase (GGT).name: Gamma Glutamyl Transferase
Gamma Glutamyl Transferase (GGT).explanation: Enzyme indicative of liver function and alcohol consumption.

Protein Total.name: Total Protein
Protein Total.explanation: Total amount of albumin and globulin in the blood.

Albumin, Serum.name: Albumin, Serum
Albumin, Serum.explanation: The most abundant protein in blood plasma, made by the liver.

Globulin.name: Globulin
Globulin.explanation: A group of proteins for immune system function.

Albumin / Globulin Ratio.name: Albumin / Globulin Ratio
Albumin / Globulin Ratio.explanation: Ratio to help identify liver, kidney or immune problems.

Fasting Plasma Glucose.name: Fasting Plasma Glucose
Fasting Plasma Glucose.explanation: Measures blood sugar level after fasting.

Glycosylated Haemoglobin (GHb/HBA1c).name: HbA1c
Glycosylated Haemoglobin (GHb/HBA1c).explanation: Average blood sugar levels over the past 3 months.

Glycated Haemoglobin (A1c).name: HbA1c
Glycated Haemoglobin (A1c).explanation: Average blood sugar levels over the past 3 months (synonym for GHb/HbA1c).

Estimated Average Glucose (eAG).name: Estimated Average Glucose
Estimated Average Glucose (eAG).explanation: Translates A1c into an average blood sugar level.

Hemoglobin.name: Hemoglobin
Hemoglobin.explanation: Protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen.

Erythrocyte Count (RBC Count).name: RBC Count
Erythrocyte Count (RBC Count).explanation: Total number of red blood cells.

Hematocrit (PCV).name: Hematocrit
Hematocrit (PCV).explanation: Percentage of blood volume occupied by red blood cells.

Platelet Count.name: Platelets
Platelet Count.explanation: Cells that help blood to clot.

Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV).name: Mean Corpuscular Volume
Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV).explanation: Average size of red blood cells.

Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin (MCH).name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin
Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin (MCH).explanation: Average amount of hemoglobin per red blood cell.

Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentratio.name: MCHC
Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentratio.explanation: Average concentration of hemoglobin in a given volume of red blood cells.

Red Blood Cell Distribution Width (RDW-C.name: RDW-CV
Red Blood Cell Distribution Width (RDW-C.explanation: Variation in size of red blood cells.

Total Leucocyte Count (WBC).name: WBC Count
Total Leucocyte Count (WBC).explanation: Total number of white blood cells, immune system function.

Neutrophils.name: Neutrophils
Neutrophils.explanation: Primary white blood cells to fight bacterial infections.

Lymphocytes.name: Lymphocytes
Lymphocytes.explanation: White blood cells important for immune response, particularly to viruses.

Eosinophils.name: Eosinophils
Eosinophils.explanation: White blood cells involved in allergic reactions and against parasites.

Monocytes.name: Monocytes
Monocytes.explanation: White blood cells that break down foreign materials.

Basophils.name: Basophils
Basophils.explanation: White blood cells involved in allergic and inflammatory responses.

RBC Morphology.name: RBC Morphology
RBC Morphology.explanation: Describes the shape and appearance of red blood cells.

WBC Morphology.name: WBC Morphology
WBC Morphology.explanation: Describes the state and maturity of white blood cells.

Platelet Morphology.name: Platelet Morphology
Platelet Morphology.explanation: Describes the size and appearance of platelets.

Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate (ESR).name: ESR
Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate (ESR).explanation: A general marker for inflammation in the body.

pH.name: Urine pH
pH.explanation: Measures the acidity or alkalinity of urine.

Specific Gravity.name: Specific Gravity
Specific Gravity.explanation: Concentration of particles in the urine.

Protein.name: Urine Protein
Protein.explanation: Detects presence of protein in urine (should be negative).

Glucose.name: Urine Glucose
Glucose.explanation: Detects presence of glucose in urine (should be negative).

Ketones.name: Urine Ketones
Ketones.explanation: Detects presence of ketones in urine (should be negative).

Bile Salts.name: Urine Bile Salts
Bile Salts.explanation: Indicator of liver or biliary tract disease.

Nitrite.name: Urine Nitrite
Nitrite.explanation: Sign of a bacterial infection in the urinary tract.

Blood.name: Urine Blood
Blood.explanation: Detects blood cells or hemoglobin in urine.

Leukocytes.name: Urine Leukocytes
Leukocytes.explanation: Indicates white blood cells in urine (infection).

Bilirubin.name: Urine Bilirubin
Bilirubin.explanation: Should be negative; presence indicates liver problem.

PUS Cells.name: PUS Cells
PUS Cells.explanation: White blood cells in urine viewed under a microscope.

RBC Cells.name: RBC Cells (Microscopic)
RBC Cells.explanation: Red blood cells in urine viewed under a microscope.

Epithelial Cells.name: Epithelial Cells
Epithelial Cells.explanation: Cells from the lining of the urinary tract.

Casts.name: Urinary Casts
Casts.explanation: Cylindrical structures formed in the kidneys.

Crystals.name: Urinary Crystals
Crystals.explanation: Can indicate kidney stones or metabolic issues.
